---
title: "HERS data"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{HERS data}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(rmb)
```

This vignette implements an expanded, dataset-organized RMB2e analysis workflow for `hers`.

## Study design

```{r study-design}
data(rmb_datasets, package = "rmb")
rmb_datasets$study_design[rmb_datasets$object == "hers"]
```


```{r load-hers}
data(hers, package = "rmb")
dat <- hers
dim(dat)
head(dat)
summary(dat)
```

## Variable labels

```{r labels}
var_labels <- vapply(dat, function(x) {
  lbl <- attr(x, "label")
  if (is.null(lbl) || length(lbl) == 0 || all(lbl == "")) NA_character_ else paste(as.character(lbl), collapse = "; ")
}, character(1))
stats::setNames(var_labels, names(dat))
```

## RMB2e analysis families

```{r analyses, results='asis'}
run_rmb_dataset_analyses <- function(dat, dataset_name) {
  integer_tolerance <- 1e-8
  min_stable_fit_n <- 20
  min_unique_continuous <- 10

  is_binary <- function(x) {
    x <- stats::na.omit(x)
    if (length(x) == 0) {
      return(FALSE)
    }
    if (is.logical(x)) {
      return(TRUE)
    }
    ux <- unique(x)
    if (length(ux) > 2) {
      return(FALSE)
    }
    if (is.factor(x)) {
      return(TRUE)
    }
    is.numeric(x) && all(ux %in% c(0, 1))
  }

  is_count <- function(x) {
    if (!is.numeric(x)) {
      return(FALSE)
    }
    x <- stats::na.omit(x)
    if (length(x) == 0) {
      return(FALSE)
    }
    all(x >= 0 & abs(x - round(x)) < integer_tolerance) &&
      length(unique(x)) > 2
  }

  usable_predictors <- function(df, exclude = character()) {
    candidates <- names(df)[vapply(df, function(x) {
      is.numeric(x) || is.factor(x) || is.logical(x)
    }, logical(1))]
    setdiff(candidates, exclude)
  }

  run_model <- function(
    label,
    response,
    family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson")
  ) {
    family <- match.arg(family)
    predictors <- usable_predictors(dat, exclude = response)
    if (length(predictors) == 0) {
      cat(
        "\n### ", label,
        "\n\nNo compatible predictors available.\n",
        sep = ""
      )
      return(invisible(NULL))
    }

    predictor <- predictors[1]
    df <- dat[, c(response, predictor), drop = FALSE]
    names(df) <- c(".y", ".x")
    df <- stats::na.omit(df)

    if (nrow(df) < min_stable_fit_n) {
      cat(
        "\n### ", label,
        "\n\nNot enough complete observations for a stable fit.\n",
        sep = ""
      )
      return(invisible(NULL))
    }

    if (is.logical(df$.x) || is.character(df$.x)) {
      df$.x <- as.factor(df$.x)
    }

    fit <- switch(
      family,
      gaussian = stats::lm(.y ~ .x, data = df),
      binomial = stats::glm(
        .y ~ .x,
        data = df,
        family = stats::binomial()
      ),
      poisson = stats::glm(.y ~ .x, data = df, family = stats::poisson())
    )

    cat("\n### ", label, "\n\n", sep = "")
    print(summary(fit))
  }

  cat(
    "This section runs expanded analyses for `",
    dataset_name,
    "` across core RMB2e model families when the data support each model.\n\n",
    sep = ""
  )

  continuous_responses <- names(dat)[vapply(dat, function(x) {
    is.numeric(x) &&
      length(unique(stats::na.omit(x))) > min_unique_continuous
  }, logical(1))]

  if (length(continuous_responses) > 0) {
    run_model(
      "Linear regression",
      continuous_responses[1],
      family = "gaussian"
    )
  } else {
    cat(
      "\n### Linear regression\n\n",
      "No suitable continuous response was found.\n",
      sep = ""
    )
  }

  binary_responses <- names(dat)[vapply(dat, is_binary, logical(1))]
  if (length(binary_responses) > 0) {
    run_model("Logistic regression", binary_responses[1], family = "binomial")
  } else {
    cat("\n### Logistic regression\n\nNo suitable binary response was found.\n")
  }

  count_responses <- names(dat)[vapply(dat, is_count, logical(1))]
  if (length(count_responses) > 0) {
    run_model("Poisson regression", count_responses[1], family = "poisson")
  } else {
    cat("\n### Poisson regression\n\nNo suitable count response was found.\n")
  }

  # `requireNamespace()` loads the namespace; we call functions via `survival::`.
  if (requireNamespace("survival", quietly = TRUE)) {
    time_candidates <- grep("time|days|day|follow|futime|surv", names(dat),
      ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE
    )
    event_candidates <- grep("status|event|death|died|fail|censor", names(dat),
      ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE
    )

    if (length(time_candidates) > 0 && length(event_candidates) > 0) {
      time_var <- time_candidates[1]
      event_var <- event_candidates[1]

      event_raw <- dat[[event_var]]
      if (is.factor(event_raw)) {
        event_raw <- as.integer(event_raw) - 1L
      }
      if (is.logical(event_raw)) {
        event_raw <- as.integer(event_raw)
      }

      ok <- is.numeric(dat[[time_var]]) && is.numeric(event_raw)
      if (ok) {
        df <- data.frame(
          .time = dat[[time_var]],
          .event = event_raw,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
        predictors <- usable_predictors(dat, exclude = c(time_var, event_var))

        if (length(predictors) > 0) {
          predictor <- predictors[1]
          df$.x <- dat[[predictor]]
          df <- stats::na.omit(df)

          if (nrow(df) >= min_stable_fit_n && all(df$.event %in% c(0, 1))) {
            if (is.logical(df$.x) || is.character(df$.x)) {
              df$.x <- as.factor(df$.x)
            }
            fit <- survival::coxph(
              survival::Surv(.time, .event) ~ .x,
              data = df
            )
            cat("\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n")
            print(summary(fit))
          } else {
            cat("\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n",
              "No suitable complete cases with binary event coding ",
              "were found.\n",
              sep = ""
            )
          }
        } else {
          cat(
            "\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n",
            "No compatible predictors available.\n",
            sep = ""
          )
        }
      } else {
        cat(
          "\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n",
          "Candidate time/event variables were not numeric.\n",
          sep = ""
        )
      }
    } else {
      cat(
        "\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n",
        "No obvious time/event variable pair was found.\n",
        sep = ""
      )
    }
  } else {
    cat(
      "\n### Cox proportional hazards model\n\n",
      "`survival` is not available in this environment.\n",
      sep = ""
    )
  }
}

run_rmb_dataset_analyses(dat, "hers")
```

## Source

- UCSF Regression Methods companion data: https://regression.ucsf.edu/sites/g/files/tkssra16191/files/wysiwyg/home/data/hersdata.dta
- Book: Vittinghoff E, Glidden DV, Shiboski SC, McCulloch CE (2012). *Regression Methods in Biostatistics* (2nd edition).
